page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring two shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Local storage is bundled as a 320 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier (top 10%), and single-threaded compression tasks show strong results, with gzip compression and bzip3 decompression placing in the top 25%. However, overall multi-core execution is more limited; the stress-ng multi-core metric falls into the average category, while the PassMark CPU score, physics test, and extended instructions test all rank in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, but small-block cached read bandwidth is weak.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in lower-tier results for highly concurrent or throughput-sensitive workloads. Static web serving throughput is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, and database operations via PassMark and Redis SET operations both rank in the bottom 25%. For machine learning, small model text generation achieves average speeds, but medium model text generation drops to the bottom 10%. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as caching layers, basic file storage, or lightweight development environments that benefit from the 16.0 GB RAM and 320 GB NVMe SSD but do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core CPU performance.
description: The Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with 2 shared vCPUs from an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 320 GB NVMe SSD. While single-core stress-ng performance ranks in the top 10% and single-threaded compression benchmarks are strong, the shared CPU allocation limits multi-core and throughput-heavy operations. PassMark CPU, physics, and database benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%, and static web serving throughput is in the bottom 10%. This resource profile offers high memory density and local storage relative to its shared compute capacity, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-optimized workloads that do not demand sustained CPU performance. It is best suited for caching, lightweight databases, and development environments.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd is a shared-CPU instance with 2 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe storage, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core throughput.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-320s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-focused workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU instance combining high memory density and NVMe storage with strong single-core execution for budget-conscious, memory-focused workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa architecture with 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Bundled 320 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression
Lower multi-core, database, and web serving throughput due to shared CPU allocation
Best suited for caching, lightweight databases, and development environments
